title: Sturdy Outdoor Side Table Stool
summary: A three-step stool/side table from 2x3s and one fence picket — with the scrap-spacer trick that insets every apron exactly 1/2" without measuring once.
description:
Three cuts lists, three steps, one genuinely useful object: a 17"-tall block that's a side table beside a chair, a stool when the patio overflows, and a plant stand the rest of the year. The frame is 2x3 — the forgotten stud-aisle size, lighter than 2x4 and chunkier than 2x2 — with a cedar picket top.

The teaching moment is the 1/2" scrap spacer: when attaching the aprons, a piece of 1/2" scrap laid against the leg sets the apron's inset automatically. No measuring, no marking, identical reveals on all eight joints. Every plan with an inset detail is secretly this trick; this one just says it out loud.

The finish schedule is also backwards from most builds, and better for it: sand all the boards, stain them, let them dry, THEN cut and build. Touch-up on cut ends only. On a piece this small the entire finishing job happens flat on sawhorses in ten minutes.

tools.note: 120 grit on everything BEFORE staining, before cutting — the plan's own order, and the right one.

materials.name: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 72"
materials.note: 51" of it — the three top boards.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 1-3/4", star drive
Wood stain
materials.note: Applied to the sanded boards before cutting.

steps.title: Prep first: sand, stain, dry, then cut
steps.body: Sand all the boards at 120, stain them, and let them dry — before any cutting. On a project this size, pre-finishing turns the finish work into ten flat minutes, and the only brushwork after assembly is dabbing the cut ends.

steps.title: Build two leg sets with the spacer trick
steps.body:
Cut the parts, then join each pair of legs with two 14" side aprons: lay a 1/2" piece of scrap against the leg face and rest the apron on it while you drive the 2-3/4" screws — the apron lands inset exactly 1/2", every joint identical, no measuring.

Build the two sets and stand them side by side to confirm they match.

steps.title: Join the sets and top it
steps.body:
Connect the leg sets with the three 13" pieces — two as aprons up top, one as a low stretcher — same spacer, same screws. Then lay the three picket tops with even gaps and screw down with the 1-3/4" screws.

Touch up the cut ends with stain, and put it next to the best chair on the porch, where it will immediately hold a drink.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #8 x 1-3/4", star drive

images.alt: Small sturdy outdoor stool with cedar picket top, used as a side table
